nodes.label: Code Comment Style
nodes.description: Preference for inline comments, documentation blocks, or minimal commenting in code.

nodes.label: Code Summary/TLDR Documentation
nodes.description: Whether developer includes a summary, TLDR, or executive overview at the beginning of files or functions.
values: Includes TLDR for complex functions only

nodes.label: Code Naming Verbosity
nodes.description: Style of naming variables, functions, and classes - verbose descriptive vs. terse abbreviated.

nodes.label: Indentation and Whitespace Style
nodes.description: Preference for indentation width, tabs vs spaces, and whitespace formatting.

nodes.label: Code Structure Preference
nodes.description: Preference for functional, object-oriented, procedural, or imperative programming paradigm.

nodes.label: Function/Method Length Preference
nodes.description: Preference for small focused functions vs larger multi-purpose functions.

nodes.label: Error Handling Style
nodes.description: Approach to error handling - try/catch blocks, explicit returns, graceful degradation, or silent failures.

nodes.label: Abstraction and Generalization
nodes.description: Tendency to create reusable abstractions and generic solutions vs solving specific problems directly.

nodes.label: Dependencies and Library Usage
nodes.description: Preference for using libraries and frameworks vs writing custom implementations.

nodes.label: Performance vs Readability Priority
nodes.description: Whether developer prioritizes code performance optimization or code readability and maintainability.

nodes.label: Testing and Code Coverage Approach
nodes.description: Stance on unit tests, integration tests, TDD, and test coverage requirements.
values: Mix of unit and integration tests

nodes.label: Refactoring Frequency and Cleanliness
nodes.description: How often code is refactored, cleaned up, and how much tolerance for technical debt.

nodes.label: Schwartz Self-Direction
nodes.description: Schwartz basic value construct: independent thought and action.

nodes.label: Schwartz Stimulation
nodes.description: Schwartz basic value construct: excitement, novelty, and challenge.

nodes.label: Schwartz Hedonism
nodes.description: Schwartz basic value construct: pleasure and sensuous gratification.

nodes.label: Schwartz Achievement
nodes.description: Schwartz basic value construct: personal success through demonstrated competence.

nodes.label: Schwartz Power
nodes.description: Schwartz basic value construct: social status, prestige, and control over resources or people.

nodes.label: Schwartz Security
nodes.description: Schwartz basic value construct: safety, harmony, and stability.

nodes.label: Schwartz Conformity
nodes.description: Schwartz basic value construct: restraint of actions likely to upset others or violate expectations.

nodes.label: Schwartz Tradition
nodes.description: Schwartz basic value construct: respect and commitment to cultural, religious, or family customs.

nodes.label: Schwartz Benevolence
nodes.description: Schwartz basic value construct: preserving and enhancing the welfare of close others.

nodes.label: Schwartz Universalism
nodes.description: Schwartz basic value construct: understanding, tolerance, and protection for all people and nature.

nodes.label: SDT Autonomy Need
nodes.description: Self-Determination Theory basic psychological need: autonomy.

nodes.label: SDT Competence Need
nodes.description: Self-Determination Theory basic psychological need: competence.

nodes.label: SDT Relatedness Need
nodes.description: Self-Determination Theory basic psychological need: relatedness.

nodes.label: Moral Foundation Care/Harm
nodes.description: Moral Foundations Theory construct: sensitivity to care versus harm.

nodes.label: Moral Foundation Fairness/Cheating
nodes.description: Moral Foundations Theory construct: sensitivity to fairness versus cheating.

nodes.label: Moral Foundation Loyalty/Betrayal
nodes.description: Moral Foundations Theory construct: sensitivity to loyalty versus betrayal.

nodes.label: Moral Foundation Authority/Subversion
nodes.description: Moral Foundations Theory construct: sensitivity to authority versus subversion.

nodes.label: Moral Foundation Sanctity/Degradation
nodes.description: Moral Foundations Theory construct: sensitivity to sanctity versus degradation.

nodes.label: Moral Foundation Liberty/Oppression
nodes.description: Moral Foundations Theory construct: sensitivity to liberty versus oppression.

nodes.label: Need for Cognition
nodes.description: Preference for engaging in and enjoying effortful thinking.

nodes.label: Need for Closure
nodes.description: Preference for firm answers, certainty, and closure under ambiguity.

nodes.label: DOSPERT Ethical Risk Tolerance
nodes.description: Domain-specific risk orientation for ethical risks.

nodes.label: DOSPERT Financial Risk Tolerance
nodes.description: Domain-specific risk orientation for financial risks.

nodes.label: DOSPERT Health/Safety Risk Tolerance
nodes.description: Domain-specific risk orientation for health and safety risks.

nodes.label: DOSPERT Recreational Risk Tolerance
nodes.description: Domain-specific risk orientation for recreational risks.

nodes.label: DOSPERT Social Risk Tolerance
nodes.description: Domain-specific risk orientation for social risks.

nodes.label: Attachment Anxiety
nodes.description: Adult attachment dimension: fear of rejection, abandonment, or partner unavailability.

nodes.label: Attachment Avoidance
nodes.description: Adult attachment dimension: discomfort with closeness, dependence, or intimacy.

nodes.label: Interpersonal Agency/Dominance
nodes.description: Interpersonal circumplex axis: agency, dominance, status, control, and assertive interpersonal stance.

nodes.label: Interpersonal Communion/Warmth
nodes.description: Interpersonal circumplex axis: communion, warmth, affiliation, friendliness, and care.

nodes.label: AI task fit: code generation
nodes.description: Readiness to use AI or agents for writing new code.

nodes.label: AI task fit: code review
nodes.description: Readiness to use AI or agents for commit review, PR review, and change critique.

nodes.label: AI task fit: codebase learning
nodes.description: Readiness to use AI or agents to understand unfamiliar repositories or existing systems.

nodes.label: AI task fit: data generation and analytics
nodes.description: Readiness to use AI or agents for predictive analytics, generated content, or synthetic data tasks.

nodes.label: AI task fit: debugging and fixing
nodes.description: Readiness to use AI or agents for debugging, diagnosis, and bug fixing.

nodes.label: AI task fit: deployment and monitoring
nodes.description: Readiness to use AI or agents for deployment, operations, monitoring, and incident follow-up.

nodes.label: AI task fit: documentation
nodes.description: Readiness to use AI or agents for documenting code or maintaining project documentation.

nodes.label: AI task fit: learning concepts
nodes.description: Readiness to use AI or agents to learn technologies, concepts, or codebase behavior.

nodes.label: AI task fit: project planning
nodes.description: Readiness to use AI or agents for task breakdown, planning, and implementation strategy.

nodes.label: AI task fit: refactoring
nodes.description: Readiness to use AI or agents for cleanup, restructuring, and behavior-preserving code changes.

nodes.label: AI task fit: search for answers
nodes.description: Readiness to use AI or agents instead of or alongside search, documentation, and Q&A sites.

nodes.label: AI task fit: testing
nodes.description: Readiness to use AI or agents for writing, updating, or reasoning about tests.

nodes.label: Bug fix scope preference
nodes.description: Preferred scope of changes when fixing bugs or defects.

nodes.label: Bug fix test behavior
nodes.description: How often the persona adds or updates tests when fixing a bug.

nodes.label: Code API design orientation
nodes.description: How the persona tends to design internal or public APIs when building software.

nodes.label: Code complexity tolerance
nodes.description: How much structural, branching, or conceptual complexity the persona tolerates in code before preferring simplification.

nodes.label: Code modularity preference
nodes.description: Preference for decomposing code into modules, components, packages, and smaller units.

nodes.label: Code observability habit
nodes.description: How often the persona adds logging, metrics, traces, or diagnostics while building software.

nodes.label: Code review participation level
nodes.description: How actively the persona participates in code review workflows.

nodes.label: Code security review habit
nodes.description: How consistently the persona reviews code for security, privacy, secrets, and abuse cases.

nodes.label: Code type system orientation
nodes.description: Preference for static types, gradual typing, dynamic typing, or boundary-only type discipline.

nodes.label: Codebase onboarding style
nodes.description: How the persona usually learns an unfamiliar repository or inherited codebase.

nodes.label: Coding agent adoption barrier
nodes.description: The main blocker that would prevent the persona from adopting a coding agent.

nodes.label: Coding agent autonomy preference
nodes.description: Preferred level of autonomy for a coding agent when planning, editing, testing, or executing tasks.

nodes.label: Coding agent context sharing comfort
nodes.description: How much project or repository context the persona is comfortable sharing with a coding agent.

nodes.label: Coding agent control expectation
nodes.description: How much explicit control the persona expects before a coding agent changes files or runs commands.
values: Agent may edit within scoped files
Agent may run broad tasks autonomously

nodes.label: Coding agent explanation preference
nodes.description: How much explanation the persona wants from a coding agent while it works.

nodes.label: Coding agent failure tolerance
nodes.description: How much imperfect output, rework, or rollback the persona tolerates from an agent.

nodes.label: Coding agent memory preference
nodes.description: Preference for whether a coding agent should remember projects, decisions, or working style across sessions.

nodes.label: Coding agent security expectation
nodes.description: Expected level of security, privacy, and secret-handling awareness from a coding agent.
values: Security review only for sensitive areas

nodes.label: Coding agent tool integration preference
nodes.description: Preferred product surface or integration point for a coding agent.

nodes.label: Coding agent usage frequency
nodes.description: How often the persona uses AI agents for development or adjacent technical work.

nodes.label: Coding agent workflow impact
nodes.description: How much AI agents have changed the persona's development workflow.

nodes.label: Coding AI complexity comfort
nodes.description: How comfortable the persona is delegating complex development tasks to AI tools.

nodes.label: Coding AI job threat perception
nodes.description: Whether the persona perceives AI as a threat to their current or future developer role.

nodes.label: Coding AI learning source
nodes.description: Primary route through which the persona learned AI-enabled coding tools or AI programming practices.

nodes.label: Coding AI output trust
nodes.description: How much the persona trusts AI-generated development output before relying on it.

nodes.label: Coding AI sentiment
nodes.description: Overall stance toward using AI tools as part of a development workflow.

nodes.label: Coding AI usage frequency
nodes.description: How often the persona uses AI tools in the software development process.

nodes.label: Coding tool AI capability importance
nodes.description: How strongly AI integration or agent capability attracts the persona to a development tool.

nodes.label: Coding tool alternative sensitivity
nodes.description: How strongly the availability of better alternatives affects the persona's willingness to adopt a tool.

nodes.label: Coding tool API completeness importance
nodes.description: How much robust and complete APIs influence the persona to endorse a technology.

nodes.label: Coding tool ethics blocker
nodes.description: How strongly ethical concerns cause the persona to reject a technology or AI coding product.

nodes.label: Coding tool obsolescence blocker
nodes.description: How strongly outdated or obsolete technology causes the persona to reject a tool.

nodes.label: Coding tool open-source importance
nodes.description: How much open-source status or open-source ecosystem connection attracts the persona to a tool.

nodes.label: Coding tool reliability and latency importance
nodes.description: How much reliability, availability, and low latency matter when the persona evaluates a tool.

nodes.label: Coding tool security and privacy blocker
nodes.description: How strongly security, privacy, or compliance concerns cause the persona to reject a tool.

nodes.label: Commit cadence style
nodes.description: Typical commit size and rhythm inferred from development history style.

nodes.label: Debugging strategy
nodes.description: The persona's dominant approach when diagnosing broken code or unclear behavior.

nodes.label: Developer contribution context
nodes.description: The context in which the persona most often writes or influences code.

nodes.label: Individual contributor vs manager
nodes.description: Whether the persona primarily contributes directly, leads technically, or manages people.

nodes.label: Primary developer work surface
nodes.description: Where the persona primarily performs coding work and interacts with coding tools.

nodes.label: Developer professional status
nodes.description: Whether coding is the persona's profession, learning path, hobby, or adjacent technical activity.

nodes.label: Developer role archetype
nodes.description: Primary software role or developer identity, inspired by developer survey role categories.

nodes.label: Developer toolchain complexity
nodes.description: How many distinct software tools and platforms the persona regularly uses in development work.

nodes.label: Developer help-seeking preference
nodes.description: The persona's first instinct when stuck on a coding problem.

nodes.label: Developer problem profile
nodes.description: The kind of coding problems the persona most often seeks help with.

nodes.label: Future developer skill belief
nodes.description: Skill the persona believes will remain valuable as AI coding tools become more capable.

nodes.label: GitHub contribution mode
nodes.description: Dominant type of GitHub-style development behavior.

nodes.label: Human help boundary for AI coding
nodes.description: When the persona still prefers human help even if AI tools can perform many coding tasks.
values: When context is hard to explain

nodes.label: Issue engagement style
nodes.description: How the persona tends to engage with issues, bug reports, and feature discussions.

nodes.label: Open-source activity level
nodes.description: Overall level of observable open-source participation, inspired by GitHub public event data.

nodes.label: Pull request collaboration style
nodes.description: Typical pull request size, cadence, and collaboration style.

nodes.label: Release maintenance orientation
nodes.description: How involved the persona is in releases, maintenance, dependency updates, or operational ownership.

nodes.label: Stack Overflow participation style
nodes.description: How the persona participates in Q&A communities beyond reading search results.

nodes.label: Stack Overflow usage pattern
nodes.description: How often the persona uses Stack Overflow or similar Q&A sites in developer work.
